themes.title: Trump’s White House Correspondents’ Dinner shooting dominates global right-wing coverage
themes.blurb: The attempted shooting at the White House Correspondents’ Dinner, where President Trump was present, has become a dominant transnational story in right-wing media. Coverage spans multiple countries, with leaders and parties condemning the violence and expressing solidarity with Trump. The incident is framed as part of a broader pattern of political violence and security failures.

countries.name: 🇺🇸 MAGA / Trump
countries.note: Intense coverage of Trump's evacuation, suspect details, and security lapses; framed as third assassination attempt.

countries.name: 🇮🇹 Fratelli d'Italia / Lega
countries.note: Meloni condemns political violence, expresses solidarity with Trump, and links incident to broader democratic threats.

countries.name: 🇫🇷 Rassemblement National
countries.note: Condemns attack, emphasizes Trump's leadership, and warns of rising political extremism.

countries.name: 🇬🇧 Reform UK
countries.note: Highlights security failures, criticizes mainstream media's response, and ties incident to broader anti-establishment rhetoric.

countries.name: 🇪🇸 Vox
countries.note: Frames incident as evidence of left-wing violence, demands stronger security measures, and ties to anti-immigration rhetoric.

countries.name: 🇩🇪 Alternative für Deutschland
countries.note: Focuses on security failures, criticizes U.S. political polarization, and ties to broader anti-globalist narratives.

countries.name: 🇵🇱 Prawo i Sprawiedliwość
countries.note: Condemns attack, expresses solidarity with Trump, and ties incident to broader anti-democratic threats.

themes.title: Far-right parties surge in European polls
themes.blurb: Right-wing and far-right parties across Europe are experiencing a surge in polling numbers, with some reaching historic highs. This trend is particularly pronounced in Germany, where the AfD has overtaken traditional parties, and in Spain, where Vox is influencing coalition negotiations. The coverage highlights parallel electoral successes and the normalization of far-right rhetoric.

countries.name: 🇩🇪 Alternative für Deutschland
countries.note: AfD leads national polls at 28%, surpassing CDU/CSU, amid debates over extremism and coalition potential.

countries.name: 🇫🇷 Rassemblement National
countries.note: RN gains ground in Senate polls, with Bardella unveiling economic plans for 2027 presidential election.

countries.name: 🇪🇸 Vox
countries.note: Vox secures key regional pacts with PP, pushing 'national priority' policies and gaining influence in Andalusia.

countries.name: 🇸🇪 Sverigedemokraterna
countries.note: SD blocks expanded congestion tax in Gothenburg, consolidating influence in Swedish government.

countries.name: 🇳🇴 Fremskrittspartiet
countries.note: FrP criticizes government housing policies, gains traction in local elections, and pushes for stricter immigration controls.

countries.name: 🇬🇧 Reform UK
countries.note: Reform UK faces internal controversies but gains in local polls, with Farage targeting Starmer and Labour.

themes.title: PP-Vox coalitions spark constitutional debates
themes.blurb: The formation of coalition governments between Spain’s Partido Popular (PP) and Vox in multiple regions has ignited constitutional debates across Europe. The 'national priority' policies, which prioritize Spanish citizens over immigrants for public services, are being scrutinized for their legality and ethical implications. This theme highlights parallel policy debates in Spain, Italy, and beyond.

countries.name: 🇪🇸 Vox
countries.note: Vox pushes 'national priority' policies in regional coalitions with PP, sparking legal and ethical debates.

countries.name: 🇪🇸 Partido Popular
countries.note: PP adopts Vox's 'national priority' stance in regional pacts, facing criticism for compromising constitutional principles.

countries.name: 🇪🇸 PSOE
countries.note: PSOE condemns PP-Vox pacts as unconstitutional, using them to mobilize progressive voters ahead of elections.

countries.name: 🇫🇷 Rassemblement National
countries.note: RN monitors PP-Vox coalitions, drawing parallels to its own policies and framing them as part of a broader European trend.

countries.name: 🇮🇹 Fratelli d'Italia / Lega
countries.note: Italian right-wing parties observe PP-Vox coalitions, debating similar policies and their implications for Italy's political landscape.

themes.title: Orbán’s resignation reshapes Hungarian politics
themes.blurb: Viktor Orbán’s decision to resign his parliamentary seat following his party’s election defeat has sent shockwaves through Hungarian politics. The rise of Péter Magyar’s Tisza Party and the potential for EU funds to be unlocked are central themes in the coverage. This story is closely watched in neighboring countries, particularly Poland, where PiS is navigating its own leadership challenges.

countries.name: 🇭🇺 Fidesz / Tisza
countries.note: Orbán resigns parliamentary seat, focuses on party reorganization; Tisza Party gains momentum with EU fund negotiations.

countries.name: 🇵🇱 Prawo i Sprawiedliwość
countries.note: PiS monitors Orbán’s resignation, drawing parallels to its own leadership struggles and internal divisions.

countries.name: 🇩🇪 Alternative für Deutschland
countries.note: AfD observes Orbán’s political decline, framing it as part of a broader shift in European right-wing politics.

countries.name: 🇪🇺 EU Relations
countries.note: EU officials engage with Tisza Party on unlocking frozen funds, highlighting rule-of-law concerns and Hungary’s economic future.

themes.title: Far-right rhetoric fuels political violence
themes.blurb: The rise of far-right rhetoric is increasingly linked to incidents of political violence across Europe and the U.S. From attacks on political offices to protests turning violent, this theme explores how inflammatory language and policies are contributing to a climate of instability. The coverage spans Italy, Spain, the UK, and the U.S., highlighting parallel concerns about extremism.

countries.name: 🇮🇹 Fratelli d'Italia / Lega
countries.note: Meloni condemns political violence during Liberation Day protests, citing attacks on Jewish Brigade and Ukrainian flags.

countries.name: 🇪🇸 Vox
countries.note: Vox offices vandalized, with party blaming left-wing extremists and linking incidents to broader political polarization.

countries.name: 🇬🇧 Reform UK
countries.note: Reform UK faces accusations of fueling extremism, with party leaders condemning political violence while defending free speech.

countries.name: 🇺🇸 MAGA / Trump
countries.note: Trump’s rhetoric linked to rising political violence, including assassination attempts and threats against officials.

countries.name: 🇫🇷 Rassemblement National
countries.note: RN condemns political violence, frames it as a threat to democracy, and ties it to broader anti-establishment narratives.
